text: The number of transistors incorporated in a chip will approximately double every 24 months.
author: Gordon Moore
source: Electronics Magazine, 'Cramming More Components onto Integrated Circuits', 1965

didItHoldUp.analysis: Moore's Law isn't really a law of physics — it's an observation about human engineering ambition. And for nearly 60 years, it held. Intel's first commercial chip in 1971 had 2,300 transistors. By 2024, Apple's M4 chip has 28 billion. That's a factor of over 12 million. No other prediction in technology history has held true over such a long period. Moore's Law became self-fulfilling: the semiconductor industry organized itself around hitting the target. It's finally slowing down, but its legacy — the exponential age of computing — reshaped civilization.
